methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $73M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

data.narrative:
LabGenius, a Series B biotech company founded by James Field, has raised $73M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private companies and 3 public comparables in the Biotech & Health sector. Direct public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales was not feasible due to the absence of revenue disclosure, typical for private-stage companies.

Two methods triangulated LabGenius's valuation. Stage-progression applied a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $73M raised proxy, implying $139M. Peer-median benchmarking across four same-stage, same-sector comparables—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yielded a median of $1.3B, with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B. The public sector median P/S of 6.1x (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) provides directional context but cannot be applied without the target's revenue.

The two-method triangulation supports a range of $118M to $1.5B, with a median of $719M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around LabGenius's stage of maturity relative to the peer cohort. Key drivers of valuation within this range include clinical or technical milestone achievement, capital efficiency relative to comparables, and positioning within the broader biotech funding environment.
